vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is severely depleted. Only P3 (Pothos) retains a visible, viable leaf. P1 (String of Nickels) and P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are effectively non-existent as living specimens.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: Bare soil, no visible foliage.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present: Pothos leaf and rabbit anchor intact.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains only soil and a sensor tag. No plant matter detected.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Black pot contains eggshell debris and a single, thin, chlorotic stem fragment that appears necrotic/desiccated.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos leaf remains stable; rabbit anchor is in position.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, damp appearance; no signs of fungal bloom, but significant lack of vegetative cover.
biome_observations.anomalies: Presence of crushed eggshells in the black pot (P2/P4) suggests a calcium-supplementation intervention that has failed to stimulate growth.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The sequence shows a steady state of decline. The stem in the black pot has lost turgor and color over the 5-day period, transitioning from a pale green to a withered, near-translucent state.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: Critical. P3 is the only surviving specimen. P1, P2, and P4 are functionally dead. The environment lacks the necessary light or biological activity to support the current occupants.
vision_report.anomalies: Eggshell debris in P2/P4; persistent use of electronic sensor tags in empty soil.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is in a state of advanced senescence. The Pothos (P3) is the sole survivor, though it appears isolated. The other pots (P1, P2, P4) are essentially empty, with only skeletal remains of stems and soil-surface debris. The intervention of eggshells has not yielded any visible recovery. The environment is static and failing.
